steps.title: Recognize percent growth as a multiplier
steps.content: The key phrase is "increases by $0.69\%$ of its value the previous year." Because the increase is based on the previous year's value, the investment is multiplied by the same factor each year: $$1+0.0069=1.0069$$ A constant multiplier repeated over time is exponential, not linear.

steps.title: Check with a Desmos sample table
steps.content: To see the pattern, start with a sample value of $100$. After one year it is $100(1.0069)=100.69$, and after two years it is about $101.3848$. Entering these in Desmos with $y_1\sim a b^{x_1}$ returns a base of about $1.0069$, confirming exponential growth.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: The base $1.0069$ is greater than $1$, so the exponential is increasing. That eliminates decreasing exponential, decreasing linear, and increasing linear. Choice C, increasing exponential, is correct.
